methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 1 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rivian is a public-stage transportation company currently valued at $20.7B following $10.5B in total capital raised. The relevant peer universe in Transportation is sparse: zero public comparables and five private peers identified (The Boring Company, Einride, Harbinger, REGENT Craft, and Vertical Aerospace). This limited comp set reflects the nascent nature of frontier transportation technologies and constrains traditional valuation triangulation.

The peer-median method is inapplicable here. A review of four same-sector peers yielded zero matches meeting same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters—falling short of the minimum three-datapoint threshold required for robust median estimation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable category error and obscure Rivian's specific capital intensity and commercialization timeline relative to its actual peer set.

The absence of narrow-filtered comparables and lack of public market analogues leaves this valuation largely supported by Rivian's own capital trajectory and market price discovery at IPO. Without sufficient peer data to triangulate via multiples-based methods, any valuation range derived from this analysis would rest on precedent rather than corroborating market signals. The key uncertainty is whether the five private peers—operating at different revenue and technology maturity levels—can meaningfully anchor Rivian's current $20.7B public valuation. Additional data on these private companies' revenues, unit economics, or recent fundraising would be required to strengthen the triangulation.
